title: Trivia — Biographies + Past Simple
subtitle: 10 multiple-choice questions on famous people (Track B-C)

items.text: Albert Einstein won the Nobel Prize in which year?
items.explanation: Einstein won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 (awarded in 1922) — not for relativity, but for the photoelectric effect. He published the theory of special relativity in 1905.

items.text: Complete: "When Malala WAS a child, she ___ to school every day."
items.explanation: The sentence is in Past Simple ("was" + past habit). "Go" becomes "WENT" — an irregular verb. "Goed" doesn't exist.
Ronaldo was born in Madeira, Portugal, in 1985. He plays for Portugal's national team, not Brazil (where Ronaldinho is from).

items.text: Which Past Simple form is CORRECT?
items.explanation: "Go" is irregular in Past Simple → "went". "Goed" is wrong. "Did go" is unnecessary (auxiliary only in questions/negatives). "Gone" is past participle, needs "have".

items.text: Greta Thunberg is famous for her activism on:
items.explanation: Greta Thunberg, born in Sweden in 2003, became famous worldwide for her climate change activism, starting with school strikes in 2018.

items.text: Choose the correct chunk: "Malala ___ the Nobel Peace Prize in 2014."
items.explanation: Past Simple of "win" is irregular: "won" (pronounced "wun"). "Winned" is not a real word.

items.text: Which question is correctly formed in Past Simple?
items.explanation: Past Simple question: question word + DID + subject + base verb. "Where DID she LIVE?" (live in base form, not lived).
Malala is widely described as brave (she risked her life for education) and determined (she continued advocating after the attack). The Nobel Committee cited both qualities.

items.text: Choose the chunk that means "someone you respect a lot":
items.explanation: "Admire" = to respect and look up to. "Know" is just to be acquainted with. "Dislike" is the opposite. "See" means to perceive visually.

items.text: Past Continuous: "I ___ a book when the phone rang."
items.explanation: Past Continuous (was/were + V-ing) describes an action in progress when something interrupted it. "I WAS READING" + Past Simple "the phone rang".
